talk_metadata.talk_title: Signaling to chromatin pathways in germinal center B cells and lymphoma

unresolved_questions: How does histone phosphorylation mechanistically lead to long‑term gene activation versus re‑repression?
What determines whether the transient on‑state is reversed or locked on by NSD2/NSD1 activity?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting epigenetic dependencies in myeloid malignancies

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: CNS penetration causing neurotoxicity, limiting therapeutic window
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted the CNS‑penetrant inhibitor was toxic in the CNS, limiting clinical utility

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: ELOVL1‑mediated very‑long‑chain fatty‑acid elongation in NRAS‑mutant AML
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker stratification for NRAS mutation status in early‑phase studies

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the compound was designed for CNS diseases and is toxic in the CNS, suggesting the chemistry may not be readily repurposable for AML
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without NRAS biomarker selection dilutes signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ELOVL1 loss depletes NRAS‑mutant AML cells (genetic data) but then concluded that the same inhibitor could be used clinically despite acknowledging CNS toxicity

key_data_gaps: No long‑term survival or OS data
Lack of in‑human efficacy data for ELOVL1 inhibition

crispro_opportunity.description: NRAS‑mutant IDH AML shows resistance to IDH inhibitors; targeting the ELOVL1‑sphingolipid‑lipid raft axis may overcome this resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "ELOVL1 is a dependency in NRAS mutant leukemias... ELOVL1 synergizes with IDH inhibition"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the metabolic‑signaling network linking NRAS, sphingolipid metabolism and lipid‑raft integrity to predict combinatorial targets

crispro_opportunity.description: Trials of ELOVL1 inhibitors lack NRAS mutation stratification, creating a biomarker gap
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "flawed enrollment criteria... missing NRAS mutation biomarker"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genomic and epigenomic data to define a predictive NRAS‑ELOVL1 signature for patient selection

cited_competitors.name: Vertex
cited_competitors.context: Provided a CNS‑penetrant ELOVL1 inhibitor used in preclinical studies

cited_competitors.name: Agios
cited_competitors.context: Developed approved IDH1/2 inhibitors referenced as standard of care

unresolved_questions: What is the precise molecular link between ELOVL1‑mediated sphingolipid synthesis and NRAS signaling?
Can non‑CNS‑penetrant ELOVL1 inhibitors be developed with acceptable safety?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Epigenetic priming to enhance T‑cell directed therapies

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HDAC + EZH2 inhibition priming for bispecific antibody therapy
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No stratification for CD20 loss or EZH2 mutation status in early studies

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Compound is early‑stage, with limited disclosed chemistry, suggesting potential for alternative HAT activators
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single, uncharacterized HAT activator for multiple combination regimens

key_data_gaps: No randomized clinical trial data for YF2
Limited long‑term safety data for triple‑drug combinations

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can identify synergistic epigenetic‑immune combinations beyond HDAC/EZH2, such as BET or DNMT inhibitors, to prime CAR‑T or bispecifics
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described successful preclinical combos of HDAC/EZH2 inhibition with checkpoint blockade and CAR‑T
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational modeling of epigenetic‑immune networks to prioritize novel partner agents

crispro_opportunity.description: Need biomarkers (e.g., CD20 loss, EZH2 mutation) to select patients for epigenetic priming before bispecific therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted trial designs lacked CD20/EZH2 biomarker stratification
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to derive predictive expression signatures for epigenetic responsiveness

cited_competitors.name: tazemetostat
cited_competitors.context: EZH2 inhibitor used in combination studies

cited_competitors.name: belinostat
cited_competitors.context: HDAC inhibitor used in combination studies

cited_competitors.name: Vertex
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned earlier for ELOVL1 inhibitor, contrasted with epigenetic priming approaches

unresolved_questions: What transcription factors specifically mediate the HAT‑induced up‑regulation of antigen presentation genes?
Can epigenetic priming overcome CD20 loss in patients with acquired resistance to bispecifics?
